mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while CPI inflation accelerates above 5% year-over-year, real rates turn deeply negative. This erodes bond yields and forces equity risk premium compression, driving positive S&P 500 returns as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives forward S&P 500 returns positively over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise and recession risk declines.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=148,611k as of 2021-11-15, near pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with lagged PAYEMS growth (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400k units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST declines > 8% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating supply-side constraints tightening without demand collapse. This creates a stagflationary squeeze that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances where INDPRO MoM growth is between 0% and 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while unemployment remains below 4.5%, signaling strong consumer demand and tight labor markets. This joint condition produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward recession risk. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2% annualized, creating a monetary policy lag where accommodation persists despite strong demand. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in extended low-rate environment supporting valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 58% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while CPI inflation accelerates, creating a stagflationary signal. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices stagflation risk and profit margin compression fears.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of instances where both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
